questions.question: What do SSMs and transformers each buy, and at what cost?
options: They are equivalent formulations

Transformers buy exact recall via attention at quadratic cost (a 256k attention map is 65B entries per head); SSMs buy linear time and CONSTANT memory via a fixed-size recurrent state — but compressing long history into fixed state leaks information, lagging on state-tracking and in-context retrieval
SSMs are always strictly better
Transformers use less memory
questions.explanation: The hybrid bet: put attention layers where exact recall matters, SSM layers everywhere else, and tune the ratio — the recipe that survived three years of scaling.

questions.question: What is the SSM recurrence, and why does it give O(1) inference?
options: h_t depends on all previous inputs directly
h_t = A·h_{t−1} + B·x_t, y_t = C·h_t — computing y_t needs only the previous state and current input, never earlier x's, so per-token memory and compute are constant regardless of sequence length
It's softmax attention in disguise
It requires the full sequence upfront
questions.explanation: Modeling quality lives in the structure of A: S4 used structured matrices evaluable as long convolutions; Mamba made A, B, C data-dependent ("selective"); Mamba-3 re-adds targeted complexity.

questions.question: What is Jamba's interleaving recipe?
options: Alternating SSM and attention 1:1
1 Transformer layer per 7 Mamba layers (l=8 group) with MoE applied every other layer (e=2) — yielding 52B total / 12B active with a 256k context that fits on a single 80GB GPU
All Mamba with one final attention
Random layer ordering per training run
questions.explanation: The sparse attention layers provide the exact-recall capability pure SSMs lack, while the Mamba majority keeps the KV cache tiny — only 1-in-8 layers grows a cache at all.

questions.question: Why does Jamba's KV cache stay small at 256k context?
options: It quantizes the cache to 2-bit

Only the attention layers (1 in 8) maintain a growing KV cache — Mamba layers carry a fixed-size state regardless of length, so the total cache is roughly an eighth of an equivalent pure-Transformer's
It evicts old tokens silently
256k contexts are never actually used
questions.explanation: That arithmetic is what puts 256k on one 80GB GPU. Jamba 1.5 scales the family to 398B total / 94B active on the same recipe.

questions.question: What do the three Mamba-3 innovations target?
options: Bigger vocabulary, longer training, more data

Exponential-trapezoidal discretization (more accurate state updates), complex-valued state spaces (richer dynamics the real-valued recurrence couldn't express), and MIMO projections (higher state-update bandwidth)
Quantization, pruning, and distillation
Three new attention mechanisms
questions.explanation: Mamba-3 (ICLR 2026) is the current-best pure-SSM baseline that future hybrids rebuild around — each innovation patches a specific known weakness of the recurrence.
